title: apocynum × lividum var. elmeri
intro: Apocynum × lividum var. elmeri, also known as Elmer's Dogbane or Elmer's Dogwood, is a hybrid plant species within the Apocynaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids like apocynin. The plant has been valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever and treating various skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Apocynum × lividum var. elmeri can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and its roots and leaves have been used in herbal medicine, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
